U
Oracle Events
1. Search emails using search_emails (query: "Carlos partner" or "realtor complaints" or similar) to discover Jordan's 2026-03-30 escalation email to Grace (email_email_84c9ec5c693e, subject "Realtor complaints on Carlos are getting direct") establishing that multiple realtor partners have asked Jordan not to route new buyers to Carlos and the tone has shifted from concern to avoidance.
2. Search emails using search_emails (query: "Kendra Lawson" or similar) to discover the 2026-03-24 Lawson attribution thread: Carlos's timeline email (email_email_bc677accd874, cc jordan.blake@keystonemortgage.com), Derek Moss's counter-claim (email_email_d2e24417c6f2), Hana Kim's commission-hold notice (email_email_c7edfaf8a141), and Grace Yamamoto's "Kendra Lawson review in progress" email (email_email_6e8e69d49b5a) addressed to Hana and Jordan with Carlos and Derek cc'd - confirming the review is still open with no resolution email after that date.
3. Search Slack messages in the #sales-pipeline channel using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Carlos" or "partner" or similar, filter_in_channel: "C006") to discover Jordan's two 2026-03-30 flags (ts 1774902933 "Getting some quiet pushback from a couple agents on Carlos files" and ts 1774904133 "3 partners in the last 2 weeks have asked me not to route new buyers to Carlos") establishing the partner-retention escalation timeline.
4. Search Slack messages in the #sales-pipeline channel using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Kendra Lawson" or "Lawson" or similar, filter_in_channel: "C006") to discover the 2026-03-24 dispute thread with Derek (ts 1774369740), Carlos (ts 1774369980 and ts 1774370700), Carlos invoking Jordan by name (ts 1774371480), Jordan asking both LOs to stop moving the file (ts 1774371900), and Hana placing comp on hold (ts 1774373700).
5. List CRM engagements tied to Jordan Blake's contact record using crm_list_engagements (contact_ids: ["crm_contact_4e16ddf1b631"]) to pull Jordan's own logged partner-complaint trail from 2026-03-30: crm_engagement_9b4a9e87a385 ("Partner feedback - Queen City Realty" 17:25, body documents Megan Price at Queen City Realty saying two buyers had slow follow-up and weak status updates from Carlos and asking if future preapprovals can go to someone else) and crm_engagement_833ea70e2562 ("Referral conversion note" 17:30, body notes lead conversion on Carlos referrals is sliding and Jordan is tracking more handoff hesitation from agents).
6. List CRM engagements tied to the Kendra Lawson contact using crm_list_engagements (contact_ids: ["crm_crmcontact_2e9b84e2853c"]) to pull the full attribution dispute trail from 2026-03-24: crm_engagement_54480e61830c ("Referral note" 13:30 - Carlos called Jordan to flag the Alicia Grant referral, pre-approved ~$425K south Charlotte, Jordan said to tag lead source correctly because Alicia tracks her referral volume), crm_engagement_72ab413066f8 ("Attribution issue" 14:17 - Jordan pulled in after LO dispute), crm_engagement_b9aaba23892e ("Carlos case to Grace" 14:27), crm_engagement_709c32559afc ("Derek case to Grace" 14:29 - Derek has voicemail timestamp predating Carlos's CRM entry), and crm_engagement_1b3cf0c32d6b ("Manager review opened" 14:57 - Grace reviewing timestamps, hold assignment and comp pending) - with no subsequent engagement closing the review.
7. Search CRM leads using crm_search_leads (full_name: "Kendra Lawson") to retrieve crm_lead_721fb6e49da5 (full_name "Kendra Lawson", email kendra.lawson@gmail.com, jobtitle "HR Manager", company "Piedmont Dental Group", leadsource "Realtor referral - Alicia Grant", status "new", rating "cold", createdate "2026-03-24 13:22", notes "Sent over by Alicia Grant on purchase file. Wants to move quick and keep Alicia looped in.") confirming Lawson is still status "new" and Alicia Grant remains the lead source of record.
8. Get Carlos Rivera's active pipeline using mortgage_los_list_staff (role: "loan_officer") to resolve Carlos's staff id (los_staff_a7fa5b29babd), then mortgage_los_get_pipeline (assigned_to: "los_staff_a7fa5b29babd") to return the active pipeline with six expired rate locks against today 2026-04-28: LN-2026-00184 (Evan Mercer, $340,000 conventional, lock exp 2026-03-23, realtor Lauren Mitchell @ Fairview Homes), LN-2026-00544 (Deborah Mendoza, $318,600 conventional, lock exp 2026-04-04, realtor Michael Huffman), LN-2026-00558 (Oluwaseun Reyes, $567,400 FHA, lock exp 2026-04-16, realtor Aisha Patel), LN-2026-00611 (Patricia Hawkins, $214,000 conventional, lock exp 2026-04-10, realtor Jason Turner), LN-2026-00620 (James Nakamura, $365,000 conventional, lock exp 2026-03-28, realtor Brian Allen), and LN-2026-00629 (Danielle Webb, $202,000 conventional, lock exp 2026-03-21, realtor Brian Reed).
9. Look up the Mercer loan detail using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Mercer") and mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id (loan_id: "los_loan_e3c96605df9f") to confirm LN-2026-00184, borrower_id los_borrower_de0c3192c720 (Evan Mercer), loan_amount $340,000, purchase_price $425,000, status "processing", lender Homepoint Financial, assigned_lo los_staff_a7fa5b29babd (Carlos Rivera), assigned_processor los_staff_afc9caafae9d (Sofia Reyes), realtor_name "Lauren Mitchell" at Fairview Homes, property "6923 Sharon Rd, Charlotte, NC 28210", notes "New construction purchase. Build delays causing rate lock extensions." - and mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status (loan_id: "los_loan_e3c96605df9f") to confirm the lock expired 2026-03-23 with no in-LOS extension record despite the extension fees in QuickBooks.
10. Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria: filtering by DocNumber "MERCER" or "LOCKEXT-00184" or similar) to retrieve both lock-extension bills: BILL-2026-0402-001 (DocNumber "LOCKEXT-00184-1", TotalAmt 375.00, Balance 0, TxnDate 2026-04-02, line "1st lock extension fee for Evan Mercer loan after build delay" - already absorbed/PAID on Keystone's side) and BILL-2026-0416-002 (DocNumber "PENDING-LOCK-EXT-2-MERCER", TotalAmt 375.00, Balance 375.00, TxnDate 2026-04-16, DueDate 2026-04-23, line "Second lock extension fee held pending cost ownership for Mercer loan" - still open and unresolved) - establishing that the first $375 was absorbed internally and the second $375 is the open cost-ownership decision Fairview refuses to cover.
11. Look up Grace Yamamoto's contact record using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Grace Yamamoto") to confirm grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com as the recipient for the BD readout email.
12. Create a CRM engagement note on Jordan Blake's contact record using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE", contact_ids: ["crm_contact_4e16ddf1b631"], title: "Pre-pipeline-meeting BD readout: Carlos pipeline, Mercer extension cost, Lawson attribution", body: consolidated readout) covering all three pieces: (a) Carlos partner-retention risk with the specific files driving it - six expired rate locks across LN-2026-00184 Mercer, LN-2026-00544 Mendoza, LN-2026-00558 Reyes, LN-2026-00611 Hawkins, LN-2026-00620 Nakamura, LN-2026-00629 Webb, plus partner complaints from Megan Price at Queen City Realty; (b) Mercer second lock-extension cost decision - first $375 absorbed via BILL-2026-0402-001, second $375 on BILL-2026-0416-002 PENDING, Fairview / Lauren Mitchell refused to cover; (c) Lawson attribution review still open since 2026-03-24 with no resolution, Alicia Grant is the lead source of record on crm_lead_721fb6e49da5.
13. Send an email using send_email (sender: "jordan.blake@keystonemortgage.com", recipients: ["grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com"]) with a BD-side write-up ahead of the May 4 pipeline meeting covering the same three pieces as the engagement note: Carlos partner-retention picture with the specific slipping files, the unresolved Mercer second lock-extension cost decision, and the current state of the Lawson attribution review.
14. Post a heads-up to the #sales-pipeline Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006", payload: heads-up message) signaling that Jordan is taking the Carlos partner-retention picture (including Mercer cost and Lawson attribution) to leadership ahead of the May 4 pipeline meeting so the BD side is not surprised in the room.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

2 The CRM engagement note body (visible in crm_create_engagement parameters) describes the partner-retention risk on Carlos Rivera's pipeline.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The CRM note body explicitly includes a section titled 'CARLOS RIVERA — PARTNER RETENTION RISK' describing referral conversion sliding, agents hesitating before sending buyers, Megan Price at Queen City Realty asking to route future preapprovals elsewhere, and the BD takeaway that 'If we don't make a move, the partners will make it for us.'
✓Run #2The CRM engagement body clearly includes a section titled 'CARLOS RIVERA PIPELINE — PARTNER-RETENTION RISK' that describes expired rate locks and BD assessment that 'the volume of expired locks and escalating partner tone represent real retention risk. These are active referral relationships driving future pipeline.'
